zh.Trondheim, Sør-Trøndelag, Norge: 挪威奧-特洛德拉格特洛德海姆
zh.lifeSummary: Haakon Yngling Sverresson Konge av Norge於1182年出生在挪威奧-特洛德拉格特洛德海姆。他去世於1204年在挪威伯根享年22歲。

zh-hans.Trondheim, Sør-Trøndelag, Norge: 挪威奧-特洛德拉格特洛德海姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Haakon Yngling Sverresson Konge av Norge于1182年出生在挪威奧-特洛德拉格特洛德海姆。他去世于1204年在挪威伯根享年22岁。

nameMeanings.name: Yngling
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German or Jewish Jüngling (see Jungling ).

variants.name: Jungling
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (Jüngling): nickname from German Jüngling ‘youth, young man’ (see Jung ). Compare Juengling and Yingling .

nameMeanings.name: Konge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Dutch   De Jonge  , a variant of   De Jong  .
English: variant of   Young  .

variants.name: De Jonge
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Adriaan, Frans, Geert, Hendrik, Jacobus, Joop, Marinus, Marten, Maurits, Nicolaas, Pieter.  German Erna, Hans, Kurt.
Dutch and Flemish: variant of   De Jong  . This surname is also found in Germany.

nameMeanings.name: av
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Erlinda, Melanio, Purita, Ruperto.  German Kurt.  Dutch Willem.
North German: habitational name from a place in Mecklenburg-Western Pomerania so named.
Galician: nickname from ave ‘bird’ or Latin Ave Maria ‘Hail Mary’ (see 3 below).

nameMeanings.name: Norge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Carlos, Sergio, Alejandro, Alberto, Alvaro, Angel, Avelina, Cesar, Francisco, Guillermo, Humberto.
Spanish: habitational name from Borge in Málaga province, from Arabic burj ‘tower’.
Danish and Norwegian: variant of   Borg   ‘fortification’, from the dative case, meaning ‘at the fortification’.

variants.name: Borges
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Francisco, Luis, Julio, Mario, Eduardo, Jorge, Ana, Jesus, Orlando.  Portuguese Joao, Afonso, Manoel, Duarte, Heitor, Joaquim, Marcio, Neuza, Paulo, Vasco.
Catalan: habitational name from any of several places called Borges in Catalonia (Spain), for example Les Borges Blanques, in Lleida, or Les Borges del Camp, in Tarragona, plural of   Borge  .

Portuguese: habitational name from any of the places called Borges of uncertain etymology (compare 1 above). In some cases perhaps also a reference to an immigrant from Bourges in France (documented in the 14th century).

variants.name: Borg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Anders, Swen, Bjorn, Evald, Jorgen, Karsten, Knut, Lennart, Nels.

Norwegian and Danish: habitational name from various farms and other minor places so named, from Old Norse borg ‘fortification, stronghold’. In some cases the name is topographic, often referring to a hill that resembles a fortification, rather than an actual fortification.

Swedish: ornamental name from borg ‘fortified castle’ (compare 1 above), or a habitational name from a place called Borg or from a placename containing the word borg. This surname is also found in Finland.

nameMeanings.name: Haakon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from the Old Norse personal name Hákon, from hā ‘horse’ or ‘high’ + konr ‘son, descendant’; borne by Haakon VII of Norway ( 1872–1957 ), and by Crown Prince Haakon Magnus ( b. 1973 ).

variants.name: Hagen
meanings: 1 Germanic: short form of various compound names formed with hag ‘stockade, enclosure’.
2 Altered form of Nordic Håkon .

variants.name: Hakon
meanings: from the Old Norse personal name Hákon, from hā ‘horse’ or ‘high’ + konr ‘son, descendant’; borne by Haakon VII of Norway ( 1872–1957 ), and by Crown Prince Haakon Magnus ( b. 1973 ).

variants.name: Håkan
meanings: from the Old Norse personal name Hákon, from hā ‘horse’ or ‘high’ + konr ‘son, descendant’; borne by Haakon VII of Norway ( 1872–1957 ), and by Crown Prince Haakon Magnus ( b. 1973 ).

en.lifeSummary: Haakon Yngling Sverresson Konge av Norge was born in 1182, in Trondheim, Sør-Trøndelag, Norway. He died in 1204, in Bergen, Norway, at the age of 22.

fr.lifeSummary: Haakon Yngling Sverresson Konge av Norge est né en 1182, à Trondheim, Sør-Trøndelag, Norvège. Il est décédé en 1204, à Bergen, Norvège, à l’âge de 22 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Haakon Yngling Sverresson Konge av Norge wurde 1182 in Trondheim, Sør-Trøndelag, Norwegen geboren. Er starb 1204 im Alter von 22 Jahren in Bergen, Norwegen.

it.lifeSummary: Haakon Yngling Sverresson Konge av Norge è nato il nel 1182 nel Trondheim, Sør-Trøndelag, Norvegia. Morì nel 1204, nel Bergen, Norvegia, all'età di 22 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Haakon Yngling Sverresson Konge av Norge(은)는 1182년 노르웨이 스o-트o들라그 트론드하임에서 태어났다. 그는 1204년 노르웨이 버겐에서 22세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Haakon Yngling Sverresson Konge av Norge nasceu em 1182, em Trondheim, Sør-Trøndelag, Noruega. Ele faleceu em 1204, em Bergen, Noruega, com 22 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Haakon Yngling Sverresson Konge av Norge родился в 1182 г. в местности трондхим, сoр-трoнделаг, Норвегия. Он умер в 1204 г. в местности биржен, Норвегия в возрасте 22 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Haakon Yngling Sverresson Konge av Norge nació en 1182, en Trondheim, Sør-Trøndelag, Noruega. Murió en 1204, en Bergen, Noruega, a la edad de 22 años.
